_units.UA_W_K: W/K - Overall heat transfer coefficient × area
_units.pump_power_W_per_kgs: W/(kg/s) - Pump power per unit flow rate

_sizing_notes.UA_sizing: UA determines heat exchange effectiveness. Q_hx = UA × (T_hot - T_cold). For stable operation: UA should be sized so that Q_hx ≈ expected heat load when ΔT is reasonable (5-15°C). Formula: UA = Q_design / ΔT_design. For 20kW load with 10°C ΔT: UA = 2000 W/K.
_sizing_notes.stability: If UA is too high relative to loop thermal masses, temperatures oscillate. Rule: UA × dt / C_loop < 0.5 for stability. With C_loop = V × ρ × c_p.
_sizing_notes.thermal_mass_ratio: CDU loop volumes should be larger than chip-level loops (10-100x) to provide thermal buffering and prevent instabilities from propagating.

small_datacenter._description: CDU for small datacenter (2-4 racks, <50kW IT load)

medium_datacenter._description: CDU for medium datacenter (8-16 racks, 100-500kW IT load)

large_datacenter._description: CDU for large datacenter (32+ racks, >1MW IT load)
